summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a strong late‑80s show highlighted by a blistering “The Stranger” and tight China > Rider, with stellar bass and keyboard work.
review: The recording is a clean, well‑balanced soundboard that lets the band’s tight late‑80s groove shine through. Highlights include an incendiary “The Stranger,” flawless Brent Mydland harmonies, Phil Lesh’s fire on “Eyes,” and a soaring “Morning Dew” to close. Minor slip-ups (a few missed words on “Althea”) don’t detract from the overall high‑energy performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently strong 4‑star reviews praise both the musical performance and the pristine soundboard quality, with only minor performance slips noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with stellar balance; reviewers repeatedly call the sound ‘great’, ‘stellar’ and note a recent CM upgrade improving fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set praised for Bird Song and a powerful Stranger; second set kicks off with China > Rider and builds to an iconic late‑80s Morning Dew finale, delivering a cohesive, high‑energy arc.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Forgot the words on Althea but still sounded awesome; delivered a white‑hot solo on The Stranger and built intensity into the final Morning Dew.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as ‘awesome’ and ‘on fire,’ especially highlighted on Eyes throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s harmonies noted as flawless across the performance.
